title: STANDARD FOR FAT SPREADS AND BLENDED SPREADS CXS 256-1999

sections.section: STANDARD FOR FAT SPREADS AND BLENDED SPREADS CXS 256-1999
sections.summary: Title of the Codex standard: 'Standard for Fat Spreads and Blended Spreads (CXS 256‑1999)', adopted 1999, revised 2007 and 2009, and amended in 2017, 2019 and 2021.

Scope: applies to fat products intended primarily as spreads with 10–90% fat, includes margarine and similar products, excludes spreads derived exclusively from milk (with only necessary added substances), excludes butter and dairy spreads, and excludes products with fat less than two‑thirds of dry matter (excluding salt).

Defines the covered products as plastic or fluid emulsions mainly of water and edible fats/oils and defines 'edible fats and oils' as glycerides of fatty acids from vegetable, animal (including milk) or marine origin, including physically/chemically modified fats, with animal fats from slaughtered animals required to come from animals healthy at slaughter and fit for human consumption as determined by a competent authority.
States that fat spreads and blended spreads are foods that are plastic or fluid emulsions principally of water and edible fats and oils.

Defines 'edible fats and oils' as glycerides of fatty acids of vegetable, animal (including milk) or marine origin that may contain small amounts of other lipids or free fatty acids, includes fats subjected to physical/chemical modification, and requires animal fats from slaughtered animals to originate from animals healthy at slaughter and deemed fit for human consumption by a competent authority.

Sets essential composition and quality factors: for fat spreads milk fat must not exceed 3% of total fat; fat-content categories require margarine to be ≥80% fat and fat spreads <80%; blended spreads have milk fat >3% and similar ≥80%/ <80% fat categories for blends and blended fat spreads; permitted additives include vitamins A, D and E (with national limits), sodium chloride, sugars and suitable edible proteins, and other ingredients or mineral additions may be authorized by national legislation.

Composition details: reiterates that fat spreads must have milk fat ≤3% of total fat and specifies fat-content thresholds with margarine ≥80% fat and fat spreads <80%, and defines blended spreads as those with milk fat >3% (with national authorities able to set higher minimums for retail sale) and analogous fat-content categories for blends and blended fat spreads.
Specifies for fat spreads that any milk fat content must be no more than 3% of total fat and defines fat-content thresholds distinguishing margarine (≥80% fat) from fat spreads (<80% fat).

sections.section: 3.1.1.1 For these products, any milk fat content must be no more than 3% of the total fat content.
sections.summary: Specifies a maximum milk fat content of 3% of total fat for the products classified as fat spreads.

sections.section: 3.1.1.2 The fat content shall be as follows:
sections.summary: Specifies fat-content thresholds: margarine must have ≥80% fat, while fat spreads must have <80% fat.
Defines the margarine class as fat spreads having a fat content of at least 80%.
Defines the fat spreads class as fat spreads having a fat content of less than 80%.

Defines blended spreads as products with milk fat exceeding 3% of total fat (subject to higher national minimums) and establishes fat-content classifications analogous to Section 3.1.1.2 (classes ≥80% and <80%).

sections.section: 3.1.2.1 These  are  blended  spreads  in  which  milk  fat  is  more  than  3%  of  the  total  fat  content.  However  a  higher minimum percentage of milk fat may be specified in accordance with the requirements of the country of the retail sale.
sections.summary: Defines blended spreads as those in which milk fat is more than 3% of total fat, with the possibility that a higher minimum milk-fat percentage may be required by the country of retail sale.

sections.section: 3.1.2.2 The fat content shall be as follows:
sections.summary: Specifies that fat-content classes apply to blended spreads, distinguishing categories (blends and blended fat spreads) by fat content thresholds of ≥80% and <80%.
Section heading for the 'Blends' category; no substantive criteria are provided in this line alone.
Classifies 'blended fat spread' into fat-content categories with thresholds of ≥80% and <80%.

Permits addition of vitamins (A, D, E and their esters) with national legislation to set maximum/minimum levels or prohibitions, and allows sodium chloride, sugars, suitable edible proteins, while noting that other ingredients including minerals may be permitted by national law; includes a note on the term 'margarine' usage in some cases.

sections.section: 3.2.1 The following substances may be added
sections.summary: Lists permitted additions including vitamins A, D and E (and their esters) and states that national legislation should establish maximum and minimum levels (and may prohibit particular vitamins), and also allows sodium chloride, sugars and suitable edible proteins.

sections.section: 3.2.2 Use of other ingredients, including minerals, may be permitted in national legislation
sections.summary: States that national legislation may permit use of other ingredients, including minerals.

Permits use of acidity regulators, antifoaming agents, antioxidants, colours, emulsifiers, flavour enhancers, preservatives, stabilizers and thickeners in accordance with Tables 1–2 and packaging gases in accordance with Table 3 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) for food category 02.2.2, and requires flavourings to comply with the Guidelines for the Use of Flavourings (CXG 66-2008).

Products must comply with Codex maximum limits for contaminants; meanwhile interim maximum permissible concentrations are provided for heavy metals (Lead and Arsenic 0.1 mg/kg) and pesticide residues must comply with Codex maximum residue limits.
Provides interim maximum permissible concentrations for heavy metals in these products: Lead (Pb) 0.1 mg/kg and Arsenic (As) 0.1 mg/kg, pending Codex-established limits.
Requires that products comply with maximum pesticide residue limits established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission for these commodities.

Recommends preparation and handling in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and other relevant Codex texts, and requires compliance with any microbiological criteria established under CXG 21-1997.

Requires labelling in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and related guidelines, with product names per Sections 3.1.1/3.1.2, meaningful translations, provision of non-retail container information (including name, lot and manufacturer or identifiable mark), and clear, non-misleading declaration of fat content and milk fat when present.

States that the food name on the label must follow Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.2, allowing lower-fat spreads to use qualified terms per 7.1.1 and permitting inclusion of fat/oil names generically or specifically per 7.1.2.

sections.section: 7.1.1 In accordance with requirements acceptable in the country of retail sale, fat spreads defined in section 3.1.1.2 with a fat content of less than 80% may incorporate the term 'margarine' in the name of the food, provided that the term is qualified to make clear the lower fat content.  Fat spreads with a fat content of 39 to 41% may be designated as 'Minarine' or 'Halvarine'.
sections.summary: Allows fat spreads with less than 80% fat to include the term 'margarine' in the product name provided the term is qualified to make clear the lower fat content, and permits products with 39–41% fat to be designated 'Minarine' or 'Halvarine'.

sections.section: 7.1.2 For item 3.1, the name of the product may incorporate the name of the fats and oils in a generic or specific manner.
sections.summary: Permits the product name for item 3.1 to incorporate the name of the fats and oils either generically or specifically.

Requires that non-retail containers carry or be accompanied by required labelling information, with the name of the food, lot identification and name/address of manufacturer or packer appearing on the container (though lot identification and manufacturer/packer may be replaced by an identification mark if it is clearly identifiable with accompanying documents).

Requires product labelling to indicate fat content in a manner acceptable in the country of sale and, when milk fat is present, requires the milk fat content to be indicated clearly and not misleadingly to consumers.

sections.section: 7.3.1 The product shall be labelled to indicate fat content in a manner found acceptable in the country of sale.
sections.summary: Obliges that the product be labelled to indicate fat content in a manner acceptable in the country of sale.

sections.section: 7.3.2 The milk fat content, when present shall be indicated in a manner that is clear and not misleading to the consumer.
sections.summary: Requires that milk fat content, when present, be stated in a manner that is clear and not misleading to the consumer.
States that the product should be labelled to indicate salt content in a manner acceptable in the country of retail sale.

Lists reference methods for analysis and sampling, specifying standardized analytical procedures for lead, arsenic, fat, milk fat (butyric acid), salt, and vitamins A, D and E using cited AOAC, ISO, AOCS, IDF and JAOAC methods.
Specifies determination of lead according to AOAC 994.02, ISO 12193:1994, or AOCS Ca 18c-91 (97).
Specifies determination of arsenic according to AOAC 952.13, AOAC 942.17, or AOAC 985.16.
Specifies determination of fat content according to ISO 17189 / IDF 194:2003.

sections.section: 8.4 Determination of milk fat content (Butyric acid)
sections.summary: Specifies determination of milk fat content (butyric acid) according to AOAC 990.27 or AOCS Ca 5c-87 (97).
Specifies determination of salt content according to IDF 12B:1988, ISO CD 1738, or AOAC 960.29.
Specifies determination of vitamin A content according to AOAC 985.30, AOAC 992.04, or JAOAC 1980, 63, 4.
Specifies determination of vitamin D content according to AOAC 981.17.
Specifies determination of vitamin E content according to ISO 9936:1997.
